method_atom: a design principle in which a chosen spatial coordinate transformation is translated into an equivalent spatially varying distribution of the medium's material parameters, so that wave propagation in the physical space is bent along prescribed trajectories; this exploits the form-invariance of the governing wave equations under coordinate remapping to engineer functional devices such as beam deflectors and cloaks.
object_atom: surface plasmon polaritons — electromagnetic modes bound to and propagating along a metal surface — steered by tailoring the dielectric medium placed on top of the metal.
brief_reason: the coordinate-transformation-to-material-parameter mapping and its wave-equation form-invariance are the transferable machinery, while the metal-bound surface plasmon polaritons and their dielectric overlayer are the optics-specific object.
